page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

In benchmark testing, the D96lds_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21105.0 and a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 86003.68. The Intel Xeon processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 support contribute to strong performance in extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 6253.38 GB/sec. The instance also excels in static web serving throughput, achieving a top-tier result of 110.74 GB/sec, and shows strong capabilities in Redis SET operations and database operations.

The primary architectural trade-off of the D96lds_v6 is its low memory allocation relative to its massive compute power, making it less suitable for memory-bound applications. However, this resource density provides high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The inclusion of 5669 GB of local storage offsets external storage dependencies for disk-intensive tasks. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, web serving, and database operations that fit within the 192.0 GB memory limit.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 5669 GB of local storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 86003.68 and a static web serving throughput of 110.74 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. The primary trade-off is its constrained memory capacity relative to its high compute density, which offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks. The D96lds_v6 is best suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and compute-intensive database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v6 is a 96-vCPU compute-optimized VM with 192.0 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, built for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, low-memory virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated processor cores and extensive local storage for high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
5669 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage support
Top-tier multi-core CPU and static web serving benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database operations
